description: Quick security scan on files edited by Claude Code. Surfaces only Critical and High findings inline. Opt-in — see README for activation instructions.
command:
python3 "${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/bin/codesec-scan" --path "${TOOL_RESULT_FILE_PATH}" --quick --json --severity high 2>/dev/null | python3 -c "
import json, sys
try:
    findings = json.load(sys.stdin)
except Exception:
    sys.exit(0)
if not findings:
    sys.exit(0)
crits = [f for f in findings if f.get('severity') in ('critical', 'high')]
if not crits:
    sys.exit(0)
print('\n\u26a0\ufe0f  Security findings in edited file:')
for f in crits:
    sev = f.get('severity','?').upper()
    rule = f.get('rule_id','?')
    line = f.get('line','?')
    msg = f.get('message','?')
    print(f'  [{sev}] Line {line} ({rule}): {msg}')
print('Run /code-security-guardian:security-scan to see full report and fixes.')
"
notes: This hook is DISABLED by default. To enable it, set 'enabled': true in this file,
or add it to your Claude Code settings under hooks.
The hook runs codesec-scan in --quick mode on the specific edited file only.
It only prints output if Critical or High findings are detected — silent on clean files.
Target scan time: under 2 seconds per file.
The TOOL_RESULT_FILE_PATH variable is set by Claude Code to the path of the edited file.
